label: 698c Administration of Big Thicket Preserve

children.heading: Natural and ecological integrity
children.label: a Natural and ecological integrity
children.text: The area within the boundaries depicted on the map referred to in shall be known as the Big Thicket National Preserve. Such lands shall be administered by the Secretary as a unit of the National Park System in a manner which will assure their natural and ecological integrity in perpetuity in accordance with the provisions of sections 698 to 698e of this title and with the provisions of the Act of (; ), as amended and supplemented.section 698 of this title39 Stat. 53516 U.S.C. 1–41August 25, 1916See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Limitation on construction of roads, campgrounds, etc.; rules and regulations for use of Federal lands and waters
children.label: b Limitation on construction of roads, campgrounds, etc.; rules and regulations for use of Federal lands and waters

children.text: exploration for, and extraction of, oil, gas, and other minerals;
such other uses as the Secretary determines must be limited or controlled in order to carry out the purposes of sections 698 to 698e of this title.

children.heading: Hunting, fishing, and trapping authorized in accordance with applicable Federal and State laws; consultation with appropriate State agency prior to implementation of regulations restricting activities
children.label: c Hunting, fishing, and trapping authorized in accordance with applicable Federal and State laws; consultation with appropriate State agency prior to implementation of regulations restricting activities
children.text: The Secretary shall permit hunting, fishing, and trapping on lands and waters under his jurisdiction within the preserve in accordance with the applicable laws of the United States and the State of Texas, except that he may designate zones where and periods when, no hunting, fishing, trapping, or entry may be permitted for reasons of public safety, administration, floral and faunal protection and management, or public use and enjoyment. Except in emergencies, any regulations prescribing such restrictions relating to hunting, fishing, or trapping shall be put into effect only after consultation with the appropriate State agency having jurisdiction over hunting, fishing and trapping activities.
